item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. The description of concentric rotating bronze rings draws a few other SCPs to mind for me. It also seems a bit too overexplained, so there's not really much mystique to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really quite fascinating and well-written; it engages the imagination of the reader in a rather amorphic, non-linear way, allowing for quite a few theories (in regards to the purpose, execution, and conclusion of the machine and what came of it afterwards) to be conceptualized at the same time.

I personally found this article to be well authored and a worthwhile addition to the SCP main repository. Looking forward to more from you in the future. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm unfamiliar with the larger world you're writing in, but I was still able to enjoy this on its own merits.

EDIT: Also, congrats on nabbing the last 3100s slot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and at the moment I'm not certain what to suggest in order to flesh out the work.

Yeah, that's pretty much how I feel about this myself. There's nothing really wrong or bad about anything in particular, but it's still missing that secret sauce that makes a scip great and I'm not sure what, if anything, can be done about that. Maybe I need to put some mayonnaise out in the sun…

Anyway, thanks for your feedback Shaggy, both here and in chat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the individual pices of this are well-written enough (the description of the contraption and its operation are clear and thorough, and refreshingly graspable for those who might not be well-versed in site lore, and the prose has a clear and identifiable voice), I cannot say I really enjoy how it all comes together - as it's another in a long line of 'magic items with conveniently thorough and long-winded expository documents exhaustively explaining everything'.

I probably would upvote the prose as a supplementary work, but I cannot rightly say that this feels 100% complete with its excision - and at the moment I'm not certain what to suggest in order to flesh out the work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This hits a lot of my good SCP buttons, particularly the reference to a Foundation Department of Thaumaturgy (Never fails to make me a bit giddy). Solid backstory too, and the object itself gives a cool mental image. The journal entries are a little dramatic and "prosey," but that's kind of necessary given the narrative role journal entries play in SCPs, and it fits the theme of the object. The guy making it does sound like a wizard. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this was something that's been sitting in my sandbox for a couple months. I still have the feeling it isn't quite as good as it could be, but I can't seem to think of anything to make it any better, so I decided to finally post in and see how it does.

I had a lot of beta readers on this and I think I lost track of all of them, so I'll just say thanks to everyone who read over this for me.
